revenue: $23,993 million, up 6.8% YoY compared to $22,471 million in Q3 2024
net_income: $5,152 million, up 91.2% YoY compared to $2,694 million in Q3 2024
key_metrics: Research and Development Expense: $3,672 million (down from $4,952 million in Q3 2024)
qoq_changes: Total assets increased to $192.8 billion from $180.1 billion at year-end 2024
Long-term debt increased to $39.4 billion from $30.7 billion at year-end 2024
Cash and cash equivalents decreased to $18.2 billion from $24.1 billion at year-end 2024
new_risks: Execution and structural risks related to the planned separation of the Company's Orthopaedics Business
Potential failure to realize anticipated benefits from the Orthopaedics separation and ongoing restructuring actions
Increasingly aggressive challenges to patents by competitors seeking to launch generic or biosimilar products
management_tone: Bullish, driven by solid 6.8% top-line growth, a near doubling of net earnings, and active strategic portfolio management including the planned Orthopaedics separation.
analyst_note: JNJ delivered a highly profitable Q3 2025, with net income surging 91% YoY largely due to reduced R&D spending and favorable other income comparisons. The upcoming separation of the Orthopaedics business will be a major catalyst and structural shift for investors to monitor.
